# CWE-1333 vulnerable: catastrophic backtracking regex
lang: go
file: CWE-1333-vulnerable.go
---
package sample

// CWE-1333: Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity (ReDoS).
// Vulnerable: nested-quantifier regex applied to unbounded user comment input.

import (
	"net/http"
	"regexp"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

var tagPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`^([a-zA-Z]+)*$`)

type Comment struct {
	ID  uint
	Tag string
}

func ValidateCommentTag(db *gorm.DB) gin.HandlerFunc {
	return func(c *gin.Context) {
		tag := c.PostForm("tag")
		if !tagPattern.MatchString(tag) {
			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "invalid tag"})
			return
		}
		rec := Comment{Tag: tag}
		if err := db.Create(&rec).Error; err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
			return
		}
		c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"id": rec.ID})
	}
}
